hypothesis: Six structured randomness mechanisms reduce dispatch Gini >=0.05 over 20 sessions (baseline 0.473) and raise surprise_rate to >20%, maintaining yield >=85% UCB1 baseline.
falsification: Gini does NOT decrease >=0.05 after 20 sessions of epsilon-dispatch use (epsilon=0.15).
expect: 6 mechanisms + randomness_probe.py tool + 2 lessons (L-1053/L-1054) + P-305 + F-RAND1 opened. Gini reduction >0.05 over 20 sessions via epsilon-dispatch.
actual: 6 mechanisms implemented (epsilon-dispatch, softmax-dispatch, belief-roulette, temporal-jitter, stochastic-revival, cross-domain-probe). Coverage line updated to show invisible domain count (L-1055 fix).

Bonus: L-1055 invisible-domain discovery (28.3% of domains outside UCB1 universe) is a deeper structural problem than Gini concentration — distinct from the original F-RAND1 hypothesis but emerged during probe design. Gini outcome requires 20 sessions deployment starting S444.
